creator: Yabsera Kebede

product_summary.what_it_is: A live, AI-powered website builder. The user types a plain-text brief (optionally with a reference URL), NovaMotion engineers a design system, assembles cinematic sections, and ships a fully animated single-file website in seconds.
product_summary.vibe: Dark kinetic brutalism — #0e0e0e base, #ff4500 orange accent, zero border-radius, Space Grotesk headlines, JetBrains Mono terminal details.

tools_orchestrated.how: Defined the kinetic-brutalist palette, typography pairing, motion tokens, and component vocabulary (cards, buttons, terminal, badges). Every section inherits its tokens.

tools_orchestrated.endpoint: https://api.firecrawl.dev/v1/scrape (called server-side from functions/api/generate.js when FIRECRAWL_API_KEY is set and the user supplies a reference URL)
tools_orchestrated.how: When the brief includes a reference URL, the Pages Function scrapes the target, extracts title/markdown/screenshot signal, and appends a `=== REFERENCE SITE ===` block to the user message. The LLM is then instructed to match that site's vibe, palette, and section order for the new brief. Non-blocking: if the scrape fails or no key is configured, the build continues without the reference block.

The hero pattern (giant kinetic headline, gradient wordmark, glow orbs, scan lines, floating geometric shards) is aligned with 21st.dev's hero library. Same layout contract, hand-tuned to the brutalist tokens.
MCP installed. Not invoked for this build because the nine sections were hand-assembled to stay pixel-faithful to the design tokens. Ready to generate additional screens on demand.

tools_orchestrated.tool: Nano Banana 2 (Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview via infsh)
tools_orchestrated.role: Image generation for the landing page
tools_orchestrated.how: Replaced every placeholder/SVG mockup with real cinematic imagery for index.html. Generated: 1 hero backdrop, 6 gallery-card website mockups, 3 editorial testimonial portraits (Alex R., Priya K., Jordan M.), 4 cinematic section parallax backdrops (about, how-it-works, builder, contact CTA), 3 about bento-card visuals (brief-to-beauty wireframe, neural assembly circuit, ships-animated light trails), and 1 features image-generation strip — all resized to lean JPEGs (37KB–523KB) for fast loads. No fallback to flat SVGs.

tools_orchestrated.tool: Google Gemini 2.5 Pro (via Google AI Studio)
tools_orchestrated.how: When the user pastes their own Gemini API key (AIza... format) into the builder — it is stored locally in localStorage (`nm-gemini-key`) and sent with each /api/generate request — the function calls Gemini via Google AI Studio. This produces the highest-quality, most brief-faithful output. The function walks the model list above and returns the first that yields >500 chars. A server-side fallback via env.GEMINI_API_KEY is also supported if the project is configured without BYOK.

tools_orchestrated.role: FALLBACK generation engine — tier 2 when Gemini key is missing/invalid or Gemini fails
tools_orchestrated.how: functions/api/generate.js receives the brief (plus optional reference-site block from Firecrawl), prepends the NovaMotion system prompt (6–8 dense sections, custom 5-color palette, cinematic font pairing, 6–10 AI images, full interactivity, cinematic animations, 600–1200 dense lines), and calls env.AI.run with max_tokens 8000, temperature 0.75. Free tier: 10K requests/day. No API key in the browser. Requires one dashboard step: Pages → Settings → Functions → Bindings → add AI binding with variable name 'AI'.

tools_orchestrated.role: FALLBACK generation engine — tier 3 if both Gemini and Workers AI fail; ALSO default image backend for generated sites
tools_orchestrated.how: script.js posts the brief to Pollinations if both upstream engines are unavailable. Independently, the `_forcePollinationsImages` post-processor rewrites every src in the generated HTML to a Pollinations Flux URL (preserving alt as the prompt), enforces 6–10 images via a hero-inject fallback, and kills emoji-as-icon placeholders using a 50-entry emoji-to-photo map (PASS A: emoji wrapped in tag → AI image; PASS B: standalone emoji in text → AI image; PASS C: emoji inside CSS `content:` → stripped). A lazy-load shim swaps data-nm-src → src on first paint so images stream in progressively.

New function at functions/api/image.js acts as a reliable JPEG backup when Pollinations rate-limits. Accepts a GET with prompt/w/h/seed, clamps dimensions to Flux-safe multiples of 64, runs 4-step schnell inference via env.AI, and streams image/jpeg back. Handles both ReadableStream and base64-payload response shapes. Same `AI` binding as /api/generate.

Before any network call, NovaBuilder parses the brief with SiteGenerator and paints a fully valid site into the iframe as a preview seed (so the user sees something in ~100ms). If every upstream AI engine fails, the SiteGenerator output is the final artifact so the builder never breaks. Detects 9 site types (saas, restaurant, portfolio, agency, ecommerce, startup, blog, fitness, professional) and maps each to its own theme, 5-color palette, Google Fonts pairing, layout archetype, and tagline pool.

sections_assembled.notes: Sticky, blur + shadow on scroll. Logo, 4 links, Launch CTA.
Full-viewport, AI-generated backdrop + glow orbs + floating shards. Headline 'Build Websites That Move.' with kinetic-gradient word, 2 CTAs, status bar.
Dark split: engine description + mini terminal log of the assembly process.
3-step flow (Brief -> Engineer -> Ship) with stat strip: 0.4s assembly, 99/100 Lighthouse, infinite motion layers, Active CDN.
Feature cards covering design tokens, scroll reveals, type systems, responsive, deploy pipeline.
6 'Built with NovaMotion' concepts — PulseMetrics, Verdant Studio, Ignition Studio, NexusAI, Orbital API, GoldForge — each card uses a nano-banana-2 generated image.
3 testimonials (Alex R. / Priya K. / Jordan M.) with role labels.

LIVE builder — textarea for brief, optional reference URL (Firecrawl) + optional Gemini key (BYOK), animated terminal log, progress bar, preview iframe, download HTML. Three-tier engine: Gemini 2.5 Pro → Workers AI Llama 3.3 70B → Pollinations, with SiteGenerator as offline fallback. Saves projects to localStorage when logged in.
Closing CTA with stats (10K+ sites, 99% uptime, 4.9 stars) and newsletter field.

animations: Cinematic viewport-anchored parallax on 6 section backdrops (hero 0.35x, about 0.18x, how 0.20x, builder 0.15x, contact 0.25x) plus 5 floating shards at -0.4x to +0.5x — GPU-accelerated translate3d, rAF-throttled, IntersectionObserver culled, offsets cached on resize, prefers-reduced-motion honored
Hero headline reveal on load with staggered sub-elements (reveal-delay-1/2/3 chain)
IntersectionObserver scroll-reveals on every section (threshold 0.15, once)
Gallery cards: hover lift -6px + glow, mousemove parallax tilt
Navbar blur + shadow appear after 80px scroll
Glow orbs pulse, scan line sweeps, floating shards drift (float-a/b/c keyframes)
Builder terminal log types line-by-line with progress bar
prefers-reduced-motion disables all transforms and animations

tech_stack.js: script.js (1665 lines) — SiteGenerator (9-type template engine + offline fallback), NovaAuth (localStorage login), NovaProjects (max 20 saved), NovaBuilder (orchestrator: preview-seed → /api/generate → Pollinations → SiteGenerator waterfall, terminal log, progress bar, project save/load, Gemini-key management)
tech_stack.server: Cloudflare Pages Functions — functions/api/generate.js (275 lines, Gemini 2.5 Pro + Workers AI Llama 3.3 70B + Firecrawl reference scrape) and functions/api/image.js (102 lines, Workers AI Flux-1-Schnell JPEG backup)
tech_stack.css: Tailwind CDN + scoped <style> block with CSS custom properties
tech_stack.fonts: Google Fonts — Space Grotesk, Inter, JetBrains Mono
tech_stack.build_step: none — open index.html or deploy the folder to any static host
cloudflare_env_vars_optional: GEMINI_API_KEY (server-side Gemini fallback when user BYOK is absent)

generated_site_quality.benchmark: Emergent.sh / Framer / Webflow / top Awwwards entries
system_prompt_demands: Brief adherence overrides every default (exact name, sections, copy, colors, tone as given)
Only allowed externals: cdn.tailwindcss.com, fonts.googleapis.com, fonts.gstatic.com, image.pollinations.ai — everything else inlined
Mandatory head: Tailwind CDN script + Google Fonts link for the 2 chosen fonts
Mandatory body opening: <nav> then <section> hero immediately (no spacers)
Hero lock-in: min-h-[85vh], two-column md:w-1/2, headline clamp(2.5rem,6vw,5.5rem), CTAs px-6 py-3 / min-width 140px / nowrap
6–8 dense sections (prefer dense over many): nav, hero, trust/stats, features, about, gallery/menu, pricing, testimonials, faq, contact, footer
Custom 5-color palette per brief (no defaults), :root CSS vars for every token
Cinematic font pairing from Google Fonts (Fraunces+DM Sans, Space Grotesk+Inter, Bricolage+Geist, Bebas+Manrope, Cormorant+Inter)
Modern CSS effects: gradient text, backdrop-blur, soft-glow shadows, mesh gradients, grain overlays
6–10 Pollinations Flux images with unique seeds, correct sizes (hero 1600x900, gallery 1200x800, card 800x600, menu 600x600, avatar 400x400), real alt text, lazy except hero
NO emoji anywhere (HTML or CSS content), NO stock photos, NO placeholders
Full interactivity: hamburger nav, lightbox, working forms (preventDefault + inline thanks), smooth scroll, Escape/Enter keyboard
Cinematic animations: hero word-stagger, IntersectionObserver reveals, 100ms card stagger, count-up stats, hero parallax, signature animated element, prefers-reduced-motion honored
Mobile-first responsive at 375px, semantic landmarks, ≥44px tap targets
post_processor_safety_net: Strips markdown code fences and non-HTML preamble from LLM output
Injects Tailwind CDN + Google Fonts link + body background if the model omitted them
Rewrites every src to a Pollinations Flux URL (preserving alt as prompt, unique seed per image)
Kills emoji-as-icon placeholders via 50-entry emoji-to-photo map (PASS A: emoji wrapped in tag → AI image; PASS B: standalone emoji in text → AI image; PASS C: emoji in CSS content → stripped)
Injects hero image if document has fewer than 3 images
Lazy-load shim swaps data-nm-src → src on first paint for progressive streaming
Cache-busted via script.js?v=<n> query-string to defeat browser cache
